question.text: What was the role of the J.R. Varma Committee (1998) in Indian derivative market development?

answer.text: It recommended risk containment measures including margining methodology, initial margin calculations, membership criteria, net worth requirements, and real-time position monitoring
It recommended the introduction of derivatives and declaring them as securities under SCRA
It recommended the establishment of SEBI as the regulator of derivative markets
It recommended allowing foreign investors to participate in Indian derivative markets

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The J.R. Varma Committee (June 1998) focused on risk management: SPAN-like margining, initial margin methodology, membership net worth criteria, deposit requirements, and real-time monitoring. It complemented the L.C. Gupta Committee which provided the regulatory framework. **The Trap:** Declaring derivatives as securities was L.C. Gupta Committee's recommendation. SEBI was established in 1988/1992, before derivatives were introduced. FPI access came later through separate regulations. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch1 and Ch8 cover both committees. L.C. Gupta = regulatory framework, J.R. Varma = risk management. Both are foundational for all Indian derivative markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** J.R. Varma's risk management framework is still the basis of Indian derivative market risk management — SPAN margining, position limits, and real-time monitoring all trace back to these recommendations.
